titlestmt.titleproper: Leslie L. Luther, collector and compiler, Moravia area items1934
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-06-24 00:05:59 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Leslie L. Luther, collector and compiler, Moravia area items

physdesc.extent: .4 cubic feet. 3 reels positive, 2 reels negative microfilm.

did.abstract: Alphabetized tombstone inscriptions; scrapbook of newspaper clippings, sketches, and notes; genealogical data; and a copy of a journal kept by William Slade.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Alphabetized tombstone inscriptions for cemeteries in the Towns of Locke, Genoa, Moravia, Niles, Scipio, Sempronius, Summer Hill, and Venice in Cayuga County and for the Van Etten Cemetery in the Town of Skaneateles, Onondaga County, New York; scrapbook of newspaper clippings (1921-58), sketches, and notes concerning people, events, and churches and other institutions in central New York (1794); genealogical data on the Alley (Allee), Luther, Spafford, Sparks, Youngs, Thayer, and Sawyer families; and a copy of a journal (1824) kept by William Slade during a journey from Sempronius to Swansea, Massachusetts.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Leslie L. Luther, collector and compiler, Moravia area items, #1934. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Cemeteries -- New York (State) -- Scipio.
Cemeteries -- New York (State) -- Niles.
Cemeteries -- New York (State) -- Moravia.
Cemeteries -- New York (State) -- Locke.
Cemeteries -- New York (State) -- Genoa.

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial PDF finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: List of cemetery inscriptions in the Old Locke cemetery, Locke, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compiled by Leslie Luther. Four pages (carbon copy).

did.unittitle: "The Cayuga County Fair" By Alice Latham

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A two page essay describing the Cayuga County Fair during the years it was held in Moravia (carbon copy).

did.unittitle: Manville E. Kenyon to Editor of the Moravia (N.Y.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter (carbon copy) Kenyon encloses a letter from Frank A. Stryker of Buchanan, Michigan. Entire item is two pages, all of which is copied from original source

did.unittitle: List of cemetery inscriptions in the Old Locke Cemetery, Locke, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compiled and transcribed by Leslie L. Luther, Moravia, New York. Four pages, carbon copy. NOT A DUPLICATE OF # 1.

did.unittitle: List of cemetery inscriptions in an old cemetery near Moravia, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copied by Leslie Luther, 3 pages, carbon copy.

did.unittitle: Odd notes on people and events in the Moravia, New York area from 1794-1870.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written by Leslie Luther. 12 pages, carbon copy.

did.unittitle: List of cemetery inscriptions in the Bird Cemetery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21 pages, carbon copy. Compiled by Leslie Luther. Moravia, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negative photostat compiled by Leslie Luther, Moravia, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Negative photostat. Compiler not given.

did.unittitle: Excerpts from the History, Rules and Ordinances of Kelloggsville Rural Cemetery Assoc., in Moravia, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Officers and Trustees - Horton, Greenfield, Rooks, Hoyt, Smith, Richardson. 3 pp. typescript.

did.unittitle: Cemetery inscriptions from the Kelloggsville Cemetery in Cayuga Co

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copied by Leslie L. Luther of Moravia, N.Y., 25 pp. typescript. Rec'd. 11-30-.59. Ack. in letter of April 19, 1960. Town of Niles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also called "The Old Cemetery", located on the North Bank of Dry Creek, east side of Main St., Moravia, 14 pp. Also, title page and 2 pp. of historical notes. Copied by Leslie Luther, 17 pieces.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Located on Lot 98, Town of Venice, Cayuga Co. 20 pp. and title p. with explanatory note. Compiled by Leslie Luther, 1960. 21 pcs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Located in NW corner of the town of Summerhill, Lot. # 16, on "Lick Street," as shown on p. 49, New Century Atlas of Cayuga Co., 1904. Copied by L.L.L. 8 pp. typescript carbon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Old Settlers), located on macadam county rd.# 69, east side, just north of Dresserville village at foot of hill which goes to Sand Hill cemetery, as on p. 65, New Century Atlas. Copied by L.L.L. 2 pp. t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Located on Lot 74, Town of Moravia, on S. side of rd. leading from Wilson's Corners to Jug Street. Copied by L.L.L. 8 pp. t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Located in NW corner of Lot 98, town of Sempronius, on rd. to Kelloggsville, 1/2 mi. N. of Dresserville village, as on p. 65 of above atlas. Copied by L.L.L. 16 pp. t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Located on Lot 83, town of Moravia, Cayuga Co., as on pp. 47 and 51 of above atlas. Copied by L.L.L. 7 pp. t.c.

did.unittitle: Indian Mound Cemetery Inscriptions, Moravia, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copied by L. L. Luther. One red binder - typescript carbon copies - unpaged. Rec'd July 26, 1962. [85 pp.]

did.unittitle: Family cemetery, Town of Locke, Cayuga County

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 p. typescript carbon.

did.unittitle: Perry Cemetery, Town of Sempronius, Cayuga Co.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 p. typescript carbon.

did.unittitle: Atwater Cemetery, later known as Reynolds Cemetery, Town of Sempronius

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 pp. typescript carbon

did.unittitle: Monagan Cemetery, Town of Locke, Cayuga Co.,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Recopied from the Skilton Cemetery Records (see acc. #1273) 6 pp. typescript carbon.

did.unittitle: Searls Cemetery, also known as Cleveland Cemetery, Town of Sempronius

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 pp. typescript carbon

did.unittitle: Young Family Birth, Death, and Marriage Records

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Taken from family Bible of John W. Young, of Town of Sempronius. NOTE: name is spelled "Youngs" in copy of records. 1 p. typescript carbon.

did.unittitle: "Genealogical Sketches of The Alley Family of Moravia"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Mr. Luther. The account begins with William Alley (b. 1763-d. 1850). The name is thought originally to have been spelled "Allee." 24 pp. typescript carbon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Located on W. side of North Rd. out of Owasco village. Town of Skaneateles. Copied by L.L.L. 6 pp. t.c.

did.unittitle: Unnamed cemetery on Locke-North Lansing Rd.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Inscriptions of Alvey, Ashton, Edsall families). 2 pp. t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Town of Moravia. 8 pp. t.c

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Town of Locke. 2 pp. t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Town of Niles. Copied by Mrs. John Crosby and transcribed by L.L.L. 3 pp.t.c

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Town of Summerhill. 4 pp. t.c

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summerhill. 2 pp. t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Niles. 2 pp.t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 p. t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Niles. 11 pp. t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summerhill. 2 pp.t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Summerhill 2 pp.t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Niles. 7 pp.t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Niles. 5 pp.t.c.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript carbon copy; describes his trip, from Sempronius, Cayuga County, to Swansea, Massachusetts, home of his grandmother. 15 pp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From research in Cayuga Co. land records. 1 p. t. cc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Item from a scrapbook re incorporation of cemetery association. 1 p. t. cc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 pp. t. cc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 52 pp.+ title pg. typescript carbons. Copied by donor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 p. + title pg. t.c. Copied.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Town of Scipio. 1 p. + title pg. t.c. Copied.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4 pp.+ title pg. typescript carbons. Copied by donor.

did.unittitle: "The Genealogy of A Sawyer Family of Moravia, N. Y."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pamphlet: Leslie L. Luther, Moravia. 23 pp. ditto.

did.unittitle: Moravia and Its Past and Adjoining Townships.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Luther, Leslie L. Indianapolis, Indiana, The Frederic Luther Co., 1966. 534 pp. Placed on the Reading Room Reference Shelf. Noted in 62-66 Rept. Descr. Olin Library call # F129 M83 L97

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the Town of Venice. 2 pp.+ title pg. typescript carbons. Copied.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In the Town of Genoa. 2 pp. + title pg. typescript carbons. Copied

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Marian Luther Lepser (his daughter) 48 pp. typescript.

did.unittitle: "Genealogy of a Jennings Family of Moravia, N. Y. and the adjoining Town of Venice"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 15 (mimeographed ?) pages compiled by Leslie L. Luther, Moravia, N.Y. Six generations starting with Oliver Jennings, dates unknown. Terminal dates of genealogy, 1773-1952. Placed in separate bond folder in coll. 11-5-68.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 head stone In memory of Ruth White, wife of Philander White, departed this life September the 25, 1822, aged 39 years.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5 head stones John W. White, 1868-1926; Mary A. White, 1878-1949; Goldie, 1897-1914; Sidney, 1912-1918; Paul S. White, 1922-1929. 1 head stone Merick, 1816-1879; Eliza, 1824-1899. 1 head stone Josiah, born Sept. 30, 1795, died Dec. 3, 1841; Huldah his wife, born Jan. 25, 1804, died July 1, 1882.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jay C. White, 1857-1927; Sylvia J. Crouch, his wife, 1859-1926; Arthur F. White, 1878-1910; Fay S. White, 1884-1910; Charley W. White, 1881-1901. 5 head stones - Jay C.; Sylvia J.; Arthur F.; Fay S.; Charley W.

did.unittitle: Church Street, Moravia, N. Y., in the 1880's.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Alice Brockway Latham, 4 pp. typescript.

did.unittitle: The First National Bank of Moravia, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Leslie L. Luther. "For the Bank to use at completion of their new addition." 2 pp. typescript carbon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Luther. "For their use in newspaper articles." 1 P. typescript carbon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Alice Brockway Latham, Moravia, N.Y. 1 p. typescript

did.unittitle: History of Milan Baptist Church of Locke, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written by Purley Minturn and edited by Luther. 3 pp. typescript.

did.unittitle: Radiant Lodge No. 423, F. & A. M., Sempronius, New York, 1824-1828. "A Short Memoir of a Brief Life,"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Richard H. Brown. 11 pp. typescript carbon. With covering letter, Brown to Luther, Valley Stream, N.Y.,

did.unittitle: The Romantic Rockefeller - The story of Big Bill. Who lived to be ninety-six and died to become a legend.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: By Horace Coon. Information Bulletin # 116 of the Everson Manufacturing Corporation, Chicago, Illinois. 4 pp. mimeo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: From Roy Everson. 2 pp. typescript carbon.

did.unittitle: History of St. Matthew's Church, Moravia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written by Henry Gregory, a former pastor. With foreword by Leslie Luther. 11 pp. typescript carbon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 vols., borrowed for microfilming 9/59. On 1 reel reported on 7/60 inventory. Clippings on local history "Out of the Past" columns etc. from the Moravia Republican Register,

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 reel microfilm purchased from Mr. Luther, 7/59 Begun by Sylvester Marber [?] Luther; worked on by Joseph Gardner Luther and Rev. Clair Franklin Luther; and completed in Moravia by L.L.L

did.unittitle: Advertisement for Adriance, Platt & Co. Harvesting Machinery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Showing new Hudson River Cantilever Bridge at Poughkeepsie on one side and a mowing machine on other. Lithographed. Approx. 7 1/3 rd inches wide; 5 1/8th high. Placed in TRADE CARD collection.

did.unittitle: Pictures of students' rooms at Emma Willard School.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Apparently from a school announcement. 2 items.

did.unittitle: Picture of student's room at Vassar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipped from magazine. Placed in manila folder on exhibits in KJ's office.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Perry Pictures, Boston Edition. #112. L. In PICTURE collection. 1934-306P

did.unittitle: Photographs of Mrs. William Howard Taft in costumes typical of the period.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipped from magazines. 2 items. In FASHION collection.

did.unittitle: Article on the George Junior Republic by James Wilson. Entitled "Citizens in Little."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipped from The Strand Magazine. In GEORGE JUNIOR REPUBLIC accession folder, # 800.

did.unittitle: Article, "George Cary Eggleston - An Appreciation" by Rossiter Johnson and a review, "Mr. Eggleston's 'Confederate War'" by Joseph M. Rogers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Both clipped from The Book News Monthly. In EGGLESTON accession folder, # 110.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 pages. Published Quarterly. Quarter ending June, 1897. Vol. I, No. 1, Auburn, N.Y. Given to Howard Stevenson, Alumni News, for his "Vol. I, No. 1" Collection

did.unittitle: Picture (architect's drawing of facade) and floor plans for all three floors of State Education Building in Albany.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In KJ's miscellaneous information file

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clipped from magazine. Placed in AMENIA SEMINARY CATALOGUE accession folder, # 1150.

did.unittitle: Clipping from rotogravure section of THE POST STANDARD, showing bride and attendants in formal wedding attire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In FASHION Collection

did.unittitle: Complete file of newspaper THE LUTHER FAMILY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 20 issues+ copy (off-set) of letter from Leslie L. Luther to libraries and historical societies concerning the publication of a microfilm edition of THE DESCENDANTS OF CAPTAIN JOHN LUTHER, September 9, 1955, 1 pc "Devoted to the Interests of the Descendants of Captain John Luther..." [masthead]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Compiled by Luther. Rec'd 9/59., Also items from years 1921, 1955. Weather; also comets, aurora borealis. Also Omaha, St. Louis, Los Angeles

did.unittitle: One additional box of miscellaneous newspaper clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Distributed to Fashion Collection(Harper's Weekly supplement, D 23, 1905) Clipping Collection (Charles E. Hughes items, 3 pcs.; Mark Twain's 70th Birthday souvenir--pages from); to Archives (The Cornell Reading Courses, Vol. III, No. 51, Nov. 1, 1913. Rural Life Series, No. 4). Also with this material were 16 religious tracts--dispo. uncertain. Others discarded.
